notes: Anonymous *D dimension-cache block: linear dimension reading 700, all geometry on DIM/DEFPOINTS layers.

rationale.evidence: The block holds 3 LINEs on layer DIM (8EF8, 8EF9 as extension lines, 8EFA as the dimension line), two INSERTs of block DIMDOT (arrow/tick symbol), one MTEXT '\A1;700', and three POINTs on DEFPOINTS (dimension definition points). The horizontal measured span matches the text: x-span 59348.83-58648.83 = 700 equals MTEXT '700'. This 9-to-11-entity DIM+DEFPOINTS composition is the canonical exploded anatomy of an AutoCAD dimension entity.
rationale.rule: An anonymous block named *D<number> whose contents are exclusively extension/dimension lines on a dimension layer, arrowhead INSERTs, one measurement MTEXT, and DEFPOINTS points is the render cache of a DIMENSION entity, never model geometry.
rationale.counterfactual: If the block contained lines on building-fabric layers (WALL/CON/기둥) or the MTEXT value did not equal the distance between the extension lines, I would reconsider it as a plan fragment or generic annotation instead of a dimension cache.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection cannot show which DIMENSION entity owns this cache, its dimstyle, or what building element the 700 mm measurement annotates; the DIMDOT sub-block geometry is also not expanded here.

notes: Anonymous *U block of 159 uniform 5-vertex LWPOLYLINEs on xref-bound INSUL layer — insulation hatch-pattern fragment, not semantic plan geometry.

rationale.evidence: All 159 entities are LWPOLYLINEs with exactly 5 vertices each (sampled 30: handles 4376..4393), on the single layer 'X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL' — an xref-bind-renamed insulation layer from the base floor-plan drawing. The anonymous *U name marks an AutoCAD-generated block (typically from a group, wipeout, or exploded/cached hatch), and the uniform small repeating polylines match the zigzag batt-insulation pattern drawn inside wall cavities.
rationale.rule: An anonymous *U block containing only one entity type in uniform repeats on a single INSUL-suffixed bound-xref layer is a generated pattern/hatch fragment ('기타'), not a plan partial, symbol, dimension cache, or furniture.
rationale.counterfactual: If the block also carried long continuous lines on wall/column layers, door or window symbol INSERTs, or text, I would flip to '평면 부분도'; if the polylines traced closed wall outlines rather than uniform 5-vertex repeats, wall_likelihood would rise sharply.
rationale.uncertainty: No vertex coordinates or bbox are derivable from this projection, so the actual zigzag shape, extent, and placement inside wall cavities cannot be verified; insulation implies adjacency to walls, but whether any polyline coincides with a wall line is undeterminable here.
